key_insight: Latent Drift forecasts slow disease progression by learning subtle anatomical change in a compressed latent space instead of trying to generate the full MRI volume directly.
clinical_relevance: This could improve early prediction of neurodegenerative progression and help design more targeted interventions and clinical trials.
method: Learns progression as latent drift rather than pixel-level image synthesis.
Uses Finite Scalar Quantization to reduce small nuisance fluctuations while keeping consistent structural change.
Targets patient-specific longitudinal MRI forecasting with a progressive generative framework.
evidence_signal: In longitudinal 3D brain MRI experiments, Latent Drift outperformed diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines on both generative fidelity and clinically relevant metrics.
watch_item: Because the approach is trained on subtle longitudinal MRI signals, performance may depend on data quality, scan consistency, and careful validation across cohorts.
paper_title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
